frameworks.name: attentional guidance from working memory
frameworks.one_line: VWM contents automatically bias visual search toward feature-matching objects

frameworks.name: single-item special focus / privileged state account
frameworks.one_line: claim that one VWM item occupies a distinct prioritized state that alone guides attention

frameworks.name: continuous-report mixture modeling of memory fidelity
frameworks.one_line: treats per-item representational precision as a graded variable that predicts guidance

core_question: Can multiple concurrently held working-memory items guide attention, or does only the strongest-represented item do so?
key_assumptions: VWM contents can automatically bias attention toward matching features
individual items vary in representational fidelity that can be measured per trial
guidance on a trial can be attributed to a specific item
manipulations: memory load (one vs two colors held)
match between memory items and search distractors
per-item memory precision via continuous report
key_findings: reliable attentional guidance observed with both one- and two-item loads
the apparent multi-item guidance effect is explained by single-item guidance on a proportion of trials
items naturally vary in fidelity and typically only the strongest item guides attention
interpretations: no distinct prioritized VWM state is required to explain single-item guidance effects
graded representational fidelity, not a categorical focus-of-attention, governs which item drives guidance
connecting_signals: focus of attention in working memory
